name: Des Moines
tagline: Insurance Capital with Farm-to-Table Charm and Midwestern Value
description: Des Moines is one of America's most underrated FIRE destinations, combining a remarkably low cost of living with a revitalized downtown, thriving farm-to-table dining scene, and an extensive network of bike trails. As the insurance capital of the country, the city has a stable economy and a growing cultural identity. The Des Moines Riverwalk, Pappajohn Sculpture Park, and a booming East Village neighborhood make this much more than just cornfields and cubicles.

quickFacts.visa: No visa required for US citizens
quickFacts.safetyRating: High – consistently ranked among safest mid-size US cities
quickFacts.climate: Continental with hot, humid summers and cold, snowy winters

housing.description: 1BR apartment in the East Village or Sherman Hill neighborhood

dining.description: Casual dining 2x/week at local spots like Zombie Burger or Fong's Pizza

healthcare.description: ACA marketplace Silver plan through Iowa's exchange

transportation.description: Paid-off car with basic insurance; biking viable on 80+ miles of trails

entertainment.description: Biking the trails, free sculpture park visits, farmers market browsing; occasional show

1M.lifestyle: A one-bedroom in the East Village or Sherman Hill, cooking most meals at home, and biking on the city's 80-plus miles of trails instead of relying on a car. The Saturday farmers market and free Pappajohn Sculpture Park provide low-cost entertainment. Healthcare on an ACA Silver plan is the largest single expense. Des Moines has some of the lowest rents in any US metro, which gives this budget more breathing room than you would find in most cities.

housing.description: 2BR apartment downtown or a small house in Beaverdale or Ingersoll

dining.description: Dining out 3-4x/week; enjoying the growing East Village restaurant scene

healthcare.description: ACA Gold plan with dental and vision; comfortable deductible

transportation.description: Reliable car for Iowa winters, full coverage insurance, bike for summer commuting

entertainment.description: Iowa Cubs games, Des Moines Art Center membership, gym, live music at Wooly's

2M.lifestyle: A Beaverdale bungalow or a downtown apartment, dining out several times a week at the growing East Village restaurant scene, and a reliable car for Iowa winters. You can afford Iowa Cubs games, a Des Moines Art Center membership, live music at Wooly's, and a good health plan. A few trips per year are within reach. Des Moines's low cost of living means this budget provides a comfortable, well-rounded life with room for hobbies and savings.

housing.description: 3BR home in the Waterbury neighborhood or West Des Moines with a two-car garage

dining.description: Regular fine dining at Alba, Centro, and 801 Chophouse; wine tasting events

healthcare.description: Premium private health insurance with low deductibles and full dental/vision

transportation.description: Late-model SUV for winter driving, premium insurance, occasional ride shares

entertainment.description: Iowa Wild hockey, Des Moines Symphony season tickets, golf club, spa visits

3M.lifestyle: A spacious home in Waterbury or West Des Moines, regular dinners at places like Alba and Centro, and a late-model SUV for winter driving. You can afford premium health insurance, Iowa Wild hockey season tickets, Des Moines Symphony passes, a golf club membership, and regular travel. Des Moines's low costs mean $10K a month goes far here -- you live comfortably in a top neighborhood with meaningful monthly margin.

housing.description: Custom home in Glen Oaks or a luxury property in West Des Moines with acreage

dining.description: Fine dining several times per week, private chef for entertaining, farm partnerships

healthcare.description: Concierge medicine at Iowa Clinic, executive wellness, comprehensive private insurance

transportation.description: Luxury SUV, second vehicle, premium everything for Iowa's variable weather

entertainment.description: Country club membership, Drake Bulldogs booster, philanthropy events, private golf

domesticHelp.description: Weekly deep cleaning, landscaping, snow removal, seasonal yard service

luxuryExtras.description: Lake Okoboji cabin weekends, quarterly travel, art collecting, community philanthropy

5M.lifestyle: A large custom home in Glen Oaks or West Des Moines, a housekeeper who comes weekly, concierge medicine at Iowa Clinic, and a nice car. You dine at the city's best restaurants regularly, hold a country club membership, and fly business class for longer trips. Weekend trips to a Lake Okoboji cabin and regular travel are within budget. Des Moines's very low cost of living means this spending level provides a well-appointed life with substantial margin for giving and savings.

housing.description: Trophy estate in Glen Oaks or a sprawling compound in West Des Moines with land

dining.description: Full-time personal chef, exclusive wine dinners, catered events and galas

healthcare.description: Top-tier concierge practice, medical travel for specialists, full wellness team

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, private car service, chartered flights from DSM

entertainment.description: Major philanthropy, international travel, art collecting, exclusive club memberships

domesticHelp.description: Full-time property manager, housekeeper, personal assistant, groundskeeper

luxuryExtras.description: Second home at Lake Okoboji, first-class international travel, angel investing, foundation work

10M.lifestyle: A large estate in Glen Oaks or a property with acreage in West Des Moines, a cook who comes in several times a week, full-time domestic help, and top-tier concierge healthcare. You travel first class, keep multiple cars, and dine out without checking the bill. A second home at Lake Okoboji is within reach at this budget. In a metro as affordable as Des Moines, this spending level creates enormous margin -- enough for significant charitable giving, a foundation, and ongoing investment while covering every daily expense without thought.
